title: GET /api/dora response envelope
status: INTERNAL / DOCUMENTARY — NOT a frozen ADR-024 wire contract
note: DAG Decision D1 (docs/sprints/epic-5-dag.md, ratified = option (a)): this schema documents the /api/dora shape for maintainers and the conformance test (tests/unit/dashboard/test_dora_backend.py); it is NOT a src/sdlc/contracts/ StrictModel and has NO tests/contract_snapshots/v1/ snapshot. Freeze stays 7/7.
revisit_clause: Promote to the 8th ADR-024 wire contract (StrictModel + snapshot ceremony, freeze 7/7 -> 8/8) ONLY if a real external /api/dora consumer appears (CI tooling / an external DORA aggregator). See docs/sprints/epic-5-dag.md Decision D1.
description: Per-project DORA metrics (deployment_frequency, lead_time, change_failure_rate, mttr) for two rolling windows (7d, 30d), computed on demand from agent_runs.jsonl + git log and served behind a 30 s server-side cache (Story 5.1 _DoraCache). All window filters are bounded window_start <= ts <= now, so future-dated commits/runs (clock skew, or a crafted GIT_COMMITTER_DATE) are excluded from every metric.

metric_definitions.deployment_frequency: Count of merge commits per window (first-parent-merge-to-main model). If the repository has ZERO merge commits anywhere in its history (solo-dev workflow, no merge model), falls back to counting ALL commits per window. unit=deploys_per_window; also carries a per_day rate.
metric_definitions.lead_time: median(commit_ts - author_ts) in hours, over commits (merge or not) that land in the window and whose commit_ts >= author_ts — the per-commit author-to-land latency (author->land, NOT idea->production DORA lead time; near-zero outside rebase workflows — Story 5.17 should label accordingly). Commits with commit_ts < author_ts (clock skew / rebase / --date override) are excluded so a negative latency never skews the median. unit=hours.
metric_definitions.change_failure_rate: failed agent_runs divided by total agent_runs in the window. unit=ratio (0..1); also carries failed_count/total_count.
metric_definitions.mttr: mean(next success.ts - failed.ts) in hours, paired per target_path, over agent_runs in the window (an unmatched failure with no later success for the same target_path is not counted). unit=hours; also carries recovery_count.

insufficient_data_rule: A window is insufficient_data iff span(earliest_event .. now) < window_days, where earliest_event = min(all agent_run.ts, all git commit_ts). Additionally, if agent_runs.jsonl is entirely empty OR git log has zero parseable commits, ALL 4 metrics in ALL windows are insufficient_data (nothing to compute from). Otherwise, within an otherwise-sufficient window, a metric is insufficient_data individually when its own numerator/denominator subset is empty for that window (e.g. zero commits in the window for deployment_frequency/lead_time, zero agent_runs in the window for change_failure_rate, or zero failed->success pairs for mttr). When data_status is insufficient_data, value and all metric-specific detail fields are null — the dashboard renders the Story 5.7 'n/a' no-data cell (Story 5.17 consumes this field; 5.13 only emits it).
